SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.settleout

Class SettleOutPressureAnalyzer

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.settleout.SettleOutPressureAnalyzer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class SettleOutPressureAnalyzer extends Object implements Serializable
Compressor settle-out (and general gas-volume equalisation) pressure screening.

Implements the HAZOP "More Pressure" deviation that follows a compressor trip. When a compressor stops, the gas held on the low-pressure (suction) side and the gas held on the high-pressure (discharge) side equalise through the machine and across non-tight check valves to a single intermediate settle-out pressure. Because the discharge inventory is at a much higher pressure than the suction casing and suction-side piping/coolers were designed for, the settle-out pressure can exceed the suction-system design pressure and challenge its relief protection.

The analysis treats each connected gas volume as a compartment of fixed volume at a known pressure and temperature. The molar inventory of every compartment is summed (conserved), a settle-out temperature is taken (mole-weighted average by default, or user supplied), and the common settle-out pressure that fills the combined volume with the total inventory is computed. With an optional SystemInterface gas the compressibility factor Z is evaluated at each state and iterated at the settle-out condition; without it an ideal-gas basis is used.

This is a screening calculation. It assumes rigid, fully connected gas volumes, no liquid hold-up, and equilibrium at a single settle-out temperature. It does not model the equalisation transient, heat exchange with the surroundings, or check-valve leak paths.

References:

  • API STD 521 — Pressure-relieving and Depressuring Systems (settle-out / blocked-in gas)
  • API STD 617 — Axial and Centrifugal Compressors (settle-out pressure basis)
  • GPSA Engineering Data Book — compressor settle-out pressure
  • IEC 61882 — HAZOP "More Pressure" deviation
Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Constructor Details

    • SettleOutPressureAnalyzer

      public SettleOutPressureAnalyzer()

  • Method Details

    • addVolume

      public SettleOutPressureAnalyzer addVolume(String name, double volume, String volumeUnit, double pressure, String pressureUnit, double temperature, String temperatureUnit)
      Adds a connected gas volume (compartment) to the settle-out balance.
      Parameters:
      name - compartment label, for example "suction" or "discharge"
      volume - internal gas volume value in the supplied unit; must be positive
      volumeUnit - volume unit, supported values are "m3" and "litre"
      pressure - pressure value in the supplied unit; must be positive
      pressureUnit - pressure unit supported by PressureUnit, for example bara or barg
      temperature - temperature value in the supplied unit
      temperatureUnit - temperature unit supported by TemperatureUnit, for example K or C
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the volume, pressure, or temperature is non-physical

    • setGas

      public SettleOutPressureAnalyzer setGas(SystemInterface gas)
      Sets an optional real-gas SystemInterface used to evaluate the compressibility factor Z at each compartment state and at the settle-out condition. When not supplied an ideal-gas basis is used.
      Parameters:
      gas - a NeqSim fluid with components and a mixing rule already configured; may be null for ideal gas
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining

    • setSettleOutTemperature

      public SettleOutPressureAnalyzer setSettleOutTemperature(double temperature, String temperatureUnit)
      Sets the settle-out temperature. When not supplied a mole-weighted average of the compartment temperatures is used.
      Parameters:
      temperature - temperature value in the supplied unit
      temperatureUnit - temperature unit supported by TemperatureUnit, for example K or C
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the temperature is non-physical

    • setProtectedPressureRating

      public SettleOutPressureAnalyzer setProtectedPressureRating(double pressure, String pressureUnit)
      Sets the design / relief set pressure of the protected (low-pressure) system that the settle-out pressure could over-pressure, for the screening verdict.
      Parameters:
      pressure - protected-system rating value in the supplied unit; must be positive
      pressureUnit - pressure unit supported by PressureUnit
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the pressure is non-positive

    • analyze

      public SettleOutPressureResult analyze()
      Runs the settle-out balance and evaluates the screening verdict.
      Returns:
      the settle-out pressure result
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if fewer than two compartments were supplied

    • ideal

      private double ideal(double moles, double z, double temperatureK, double volumeM3)
      Computes the ideal real-gas pressure that fills a volume with a fixed molar inventory at a temperature.
      Parameters:
      moles - total moles in mol
      z - compressibility factor (dimensionless)
      temperatureK - temperature in K
      volumeM3 - total volume in m3
      Returns:
      pressure in bara

    • compressibility

      private double compressibility(double pressureBara, double temperatureK, List<String> warnings)
      Returns the compressibility factor Z at a state, using the optional NeqSim gas when available and falling back to ideal gas otherwise.
      Parameters:
      pressureBara - pressure in bara
      temperatureK - temperature in K
      warnings - warning sink for flash failures
      Returns:
      compressibility factor; 1.0 when no gas is supplied or the flash fails

    • toCubicMetre

      private double toCubicMetre(double value, String unit)
      Converts a volume to cubic metres.
      Parameters:
      value - volume value
      unit - volume unit, supported values are "m3" and "litre"
      Returns:
      volume in m3
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the unit is unsupported
